cases.title: Signup followed by login succeeds using stored adaptive hash verification
preconditions: API server running at /api/v1
Fresh unique email not yet registered
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with { email, password, displayName }
POST /api/v1/auth/login with the same email and password
GET /api/v1/me with returned login JWT
cases.expected: Register returns HTTP 200 with JWT; login returns HTTP 200 with JWT; GET /me returns matching profile; credential verification uses stored hash not plaintext comparison, proving NFR-17 adaptive hashing supports the identity/session flow

cases.title: Registration persists non-null password_hash in users table
preconditions: Auth module and users table available
Database read access to users.password_hash column
Fresh signup email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with known password value
Query users row for the new account by normalized email
cases.expected: User row exists with password_hash column populated and not null per database design; plaintext password is not stored in any users column

cases.title: Stored password_hash uses bcrypt or argon2 adaptive hash format
preconditions: Fresh participant account created via POST /auth/register
Database access to users.password_hash
steps: Register with a known plaintext password
Read password_hash from users table
Validate hash string against bcrypt ($2a$, $2b$, $2y$) or argon2 ($argon2) prefix and expected encoded length
cases.expected: password_hash matches recognized adaptive hashing algorithm prefix and structure; value is not equal to submitted plaintext; satisfies NFR-17 bcrypt-or-argon2 requirement

cases.title: hashPassword produces adaptive hash distinct from plaintext input
preconditions: Auth password module (hashPassword) available for unit test
steps: Call hashPassword with sample password string
Assert return value is not identical to input password
Assert return value matches bcrypt or argon2 hash prefix pattern
cases.expected: Output is adaptive hash string with correct algorithm prefix; plaintext password is never returned from hashing function; unit-level guarantee of NFR-17 storage policy

cases.title: Auth register and login responses never expose password or passwordHash fields
preconditions: Fresh email for registration
Existing account with known credentials for login test
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register and parse full JSON response
POST /api/v1/auth/login and parse full JSON response
Search response bodies for keys or values matching password, passwordHash, or stored hash material
cases.expected: Responses contain token and profile only; no password, passwordHash, or credential secret fields per domain model rule that passwordHash is never exposed in API responses

cases.title: GET /me profile serialization excludes password hash from user aggregate
preconditions: Authenticated participant session via login JWT
User row with password_hash in database
steps: GET /api/v1/me with valid bearer token
Parse JSON response and inspect all keys at root and nested profile object
cases.expected: Profile payload includes userId, email, displayName, and roles only; password_hash and password fields are absent; sensitive credential material is not leaked on authenticated read

cases.title: Seeded persona accounts store adaptive hashes not plaintext passwords
preconditions: Local seed data loaded with OrganizerAdmin, OrganizerStaff, and Participant personas per local development setup
Database access to users.password_hash for seeded accounts
steps: Query password_hash for each seeded credential-based user
Verify each value matches bcrypt or argon2 prefix and is not a human-readable plaintext password string
cases.expected: All seeded users comply with NFR-17; no seed script or fixture inserts plaintext passwords into users.password_hash

cases.title: verifyPassword rejects incorrect password against stored adaptive hash
preconditions: Known password_hash produced by hashPassword for reference password
steps: Call verifyPassword with correct password and stored hash
Call verifyPassword with wrong password and same stored hash
cases.expected: Correct password returns true; wrong password returns false; verification uses constant-time compare against hash without reverting to plaintext storage

cases.title: Identical passwords for different users produce distinct password_hash values
preconditions: Two fresh unique emails
Same plaintext password submitted for both registrations
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register for user A with password P
POST /api/v1/auth/register for user B with the same password P
Read password_hash for both user rows
cases.expected: Both hashes are valid adaptive hashes but not equal to each other or to plaintext P; per-user salting prevents hash reuse attacks

cases.title: Login fails when password does not match stored hash
preconditions: Existing participant registered with known password
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/login with correct email and incorrect password
Inspect HTTP status and error body
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with typed authentication error; no JWT issued; failed attempt does not reveal whether email exists beyond generic invalid-credentials messaging; hash verification rejects mismatch without exposing stored hash

cases.title: Long or complex password values are hashed before persistence
preconditions: Fresh signup email
Password at or above minimum length with special characters
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with maximum-allowed-length or complex password
Read users.password_hash for the new account
cases.expected: password_hash is adaptive hash string; full plaintext password is not stored in database column; boundary-length input still protected by NFR-17

cases.title: Duplicate email registration does not overwrite existing password_hash with new plaintext
preconditions: Existing account registered with email user@example.com and known password_hash in database
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with same email and different password
Read password_hash for original user row after rejected request
cases.expected: HTTP 409 EMAIL_ALREADY_REGISTERED; original password_hash unchanged; no second user row; conflict path does not persist attacker-supplied password in any form
